name: Voltage Drop Calculator
description: Calculates voltage drop in electrical conductors per NEC and IEC standards, with loss drivers and corrective actions.

inputs.label: Conductor Material
inputs.businessContext: Material affects resistivity; copper and aluminum are common in industrial installations.

businessContext_i18n.en: Material affects resistivity; copper and aluminum are common in industrial installations.

inputs.label: Conductor Size (AWG or kcmil)
inputs.businessContext: Cross-sectional area determines resistance per unit length.

businessContext_i18n.en: Cross-sectional area determines resistance per unit length.

inputs.label: One-Way Circuit Length
inputs.businessContext: Distance from source to load; longer runs increase voltage drop.

businessContext_i18n.en: Distance from source to load; longer runs increase voltage drop.

inputs.label: Load Current (Amperes)
inputs.businessContext: Current drawn by the load; higher current increases drop.

businessContext_i18n.en: Current drawn by the load; higher current increases drop.

inputs.label: System Voltage (Line-to-Line)
inputs.businessContext: Nominal voltage of the system; used to compute percentage drop.

businessContext_i18n.en: Nominal voltage of the system; used to compute percentage drop.

inputs.label: Phase Type
inputs.businessContext: Single-phase or three-phase affects the voltage drop formula (factor sqrt(3) for three-phase).

businessContext_i18n.en: Single-phase or three-phase affects the voltage drop formula (factor sqrt(3) for three-phase).

inputs.label: Power Factor (cos φ)
inputs.businessContext: Reactive component increases effective current; typical industrial PF is 0.8-0.95.

businessContext_i18n.en: Reactive component increases effective current; typical industrial PF is 0.8-0.95.

inputs.label: Ambient Temperature
inputs.businessContext: Temperature affects conductor resistance; higher temperature increases resistance.

businessContext_i18n.en: Temperature affects conductor resistance; higher temperature increases resistance.

rules.message: Long circuit with high current may require parallel conductors or larger size.
Voltage drop exceeds 3% recommended by NEC for branch circuits.
Voltage drop exceeds 5% recommended by NEC for feeders.
Low power factor increases voltage drop; consider power factor correction.
High ambient temperature reduces conductor ampacity; check NEC derating.

thresholds.message: Voltage drop exceeds 5% – immediate redesign recommended.
Voltage drop between 3% and 5% – consider larger conductor or shorter run.
Load current exceeds conductor ampacity – risk of overheating.

suggestedActions: Review inputs and verify results against site standards.
